sources.title: No Longer a Conspiracy Theory: Elite Openly Paying to Ingest the Blood of the Young
No longer a conspiracy theory: Elite openly paying to ingest the blood of the young to live longer

evidence_for.text: Ambrosia startup offered young blood transfusions to ~100 paying customers; Peter Thiel publicly expressed interest in parabiosis as life extension.

evidence_against.text: Stanford neuroscientist Tony Wyss-Coray stated there is 'just no clinical evidence' that young blood transfusions provide health benefits.

sources.title: Serpent seed - Wikipedia
What is the Serpent Seed doctrine?
Serpent's Seed - William Branham Historical Research

evidence_against.text: Mainstream theology holds the Bible is explicit that Adam was Cain's father; 'child of the devil' denotes spiritual rebellion, not genetics.
The doctrine was created by William Branham as a rebranding of Christian Identity theology associated with white supremacy groups in the 1950s-60s.

research_note: no external coverage found; fringe financial conspiracy with no dedicated journalism or academic treatment

sources.title: Nephilim - Wikipedia
Who Are the Nephilim?

evidence_for.text: The Book of Jubilees states that removing the Nephilim was one of God's purposes for the biblical flood, acknowledging them as a unique pre-flood population.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream biblical archaeology treats the Nephilim as mythological or referring to unusually large humans; no physical evidence of giants has been confirmed.

sources.title: The 10 Most Popular Cryptocurrency Conspiracy Theories
Bitcoin 'Conspiracy Theory' Alleges Virtual Currency is NSA or CIA Project

evidence_against.text: Bitcoin's genesis block contains an explicit anti-bank message referencing a Times headline about bank bailouts, pointing to an anti-establishment rather than bank-controlled origin.

sources.title: What Is Simulation Theory? Are We Living in a Simulation?
Simulation Theory Debunked

evidence_against.text: Simulation theory claims require 'significant further testing and verification before even being considered plausible'; researchers have published as many refutations as papers supporting digital existence.

sources.title: Seattle windshield pitting epidemic - Wikipedia
Windshield pitting incidents in Washington reach fever pitch on April 15, 1954
The Windshield-Pitting Mystery Of 1954

evidence_for.text: Close to 3,000 windshields were reported pitted in Seattle in a single day (April 15, 1954); the event is a well-documented textbook case of collective delusion.

evidence_against.text: Seattle Police crime lab concluded the damage was '5 percent hoodlum-ism and 95 percent public hysteria'; no government weapon or external cause was identified.

sources.title: North Korean ghost ships - Wikipedia
How North Korea Built a Fleet of Ghost Ships
First 'ghost ship' of 2018 washes up in Japan with dead North Koreans

evidence_for.text: Approximately 500 vessels from North Korea have appeared off Japan's coastline since 2011; 2017 saw a record 104 boats with at least 31 bodies.
Kim Jong Un's documented pressure on the military to fish farther out to sea, combined with old boats lacking GPS, directly drives crew deaths.

evidence_against.text: Investigators attribute crew deaths to overfishing-driven resource depletion and starvation at sea rather than deliberate government sacrifice operations.

sources.title: New World Order conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
The 'New World Order' - ISD Global

evidence_against.text: The FBI has found that NWO conspiracy theories motivate domestic extremists to commit violence; no evidence of organized NWO kidnapping-for-military-training programs has been documented.

sources.title: Stuxnet - Wikipedia
The Real Story of Stuxnet
What is Stuxnet?

evidence_for.text: Stuxnet was modified over time to target multiple infrastructure types beyond Iranian centrifuges, demonstrating ongoing active development by its state-actor creators.

evidence_against.text: Cybersecurity researchers have not confirmed active deployment of Stuxnet variants post-2012; documented successors (Duqu, Flame) had separate specific targets.

sources.title: Quantum mind - Wikipedia
Experiments Prepare to Test Whether Consciousness Arises from Quantum Weirdness
Quantum Entanglement in Neurons May Actually Explain Consciousness

evidence_for.text: Research published in ScienceAlert suggests quantum entanglement in neurons may play a role in consciousness, keeping the hypothesis scientifically active.

evidence_against.text: Physicist Max Tegmark argues the brain is 'too warm, wet, and noisy' for quantum states to persist, with decoherence timescales far too short for meaningful quantum processing.

sources.title: Anatoliy Golitsyn - Wikipedia
What if an ex-KGB officer predicted that the USSR would fake its own collapse
Unredacted JFK files reveal details of 'most valuable defector' in Cold War

evidence_against.text: MI5 historian Christopher Andrew described Golitsyn as an 'unreliable conspiracy theorist' who convinced Western intelligence 'they were falling victim to a vast KGB deception from which only he could save them'.

sources.title: Investigation of the phenomenology, physiology and impact of spiritually transformative experiences - kundalini awakening
Kundalini Awakening: What Are the Signs?

evidence_for.text: A peer-reviewed ScienceDirect study formally investigated the phenomenology and physiology of kundalini awakening events, finding measurable physiological and psychological changes in subjects.

sources.title: The Enigma of the Ummo Case: Letters from the Stars or a Complex Hoax?
An Immoral Experiment: The Spiritual, Political, and Ufological Significance of the UMMO Letters

evidence_against.text: Jose Luis Jordan Pena, the perpetrator, later confessed to fabricating the entire Ummo affair as an elaborate psychological and sociological hoax.

sources.title: Opposition to water fluoridation - Wikipedia
Debating Water Fluoridation Before Dr. Strangelove
The Fluoride Controversy

evidence_against.text: A 2018 review found no evidence for higher rates of hip fractures, cancer, or Down's syndrome in fluoridated areas; IQ studies used regions with endemic fluoride far exceeding drinking water levels.

sources.title: Human zoo - Wikipedia
Human Zoos - Jim Crow Museum
How Colonial Powers Presented People in 'Human Zoos'

evidence_for.text: Between 1877 and 1912 approximately 30 ethnological exhibitions were held at the Jardin zoologique d'acclimatation in Paris; the 1889 World's Fair displayed 400 indigenous people before 28 million visitors.

sources.title: What Is Simulation Theory? Are We Living in a Simulation?
'Simulation theory' brings an AI twist out of 'The Matrix'

evidence_against.text: Simulation theory claims require 'significant further testing and verification before even being considered plausible'; no evidence supports specific individuals holding reality-manipulation powers.

sources.title: Connections Between Food Additives and Psychiatric Disorders
Panics in Our Plates: Contemporary Legends and Conspiracy Theories on Food

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research identifies specific food additives (emulsifiers, synthetic flavors) that measurably alter gut microbiota and affect neuropeptide expression and mood.

evidence_against.text: Academic analysis classifies the 'deliberate spiritual suppression through food' narrative as a contemporary legend, not a documented policy.

sources.title: What is Mediumship? Understanding Connections to Spirit
11 Common Myths About Mediumship & Mediums
Lifting the Veil: The Rites and Rituals of Secret Society Clubs
Secret societies that may have committed murders

evidence_for.text: Documented cases include Nigerian campus confraternities responsible for an estimated 250 murders in a decade, and the Thuggee cult who killed thousands as ritual sacrifices.

sources.title: When Prophecy Fails - Wikipedia
Cognitive Dissonance & the May 21st Apocalypse

evidence_against.text: Festinger's classic study found failed-prophecy believers doubled down through cognitive dissonance; the alternate-timeline explanation is a rationalization mechanism, not evidence of real intervention.

sources.title: Technological singularity - Wikipedia
How AGI became the most consequential conspiracy theory of our time

evidence_against.text: MIT Technology Review concludes that belief in secret AGI functions as a conspiracy theory serving Silicon Valley profit motives; no credible evidence of a secretly achieved singularity exists.

sources.title: Synchronicity - Wikipedia
Carl Jung's Theory of Meaningful Coincidences

evidence_against.text: Scientists attribute synchronistic experiences to confirmation bias and the human mind's innate pattern-recognition tendency rather than any intentional orchestration.

sources.title: New Conspiracy Theory: Children Kidnapped for Mars Slave Colony
NASA denies Infowars claim that the agency runs a child slave colony on Mars
NASA Swears They Aren't Hiding Slave Children on Mars

evidence_against.text: NASA officially stated 'There are no humans on Mars'; the theory's claimed 20-year travel time is physically impossible given the actual 6-9 month transit.

sources.title: New World Order conspiracy theory - Wikipedia
The 'New World Order' - ISD Global
Ley line - Wikipedia
Dowsing - Wikipedia
Water Dowsing
Is there any scientific evidence for dowsing?

evidence_against.text: 30 expert dowsers tested in a 1990 controlled study in Kassel, Germany failed to outperform random chance locating buried water pipes.
The USGS states controlled scientific studies have 'repeatedly found that water dowsing does not work' and attribute rod movement to the ideomotor effect.

research_note: no external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only

sources.title: Pre-Columbian agriculture in the Amazon Basin - Wikipedia
Intentional creation of carbon-rich dark earth soils in the Amazon
Terra preta - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research in Science Advances (2023) confirms Amazonian peoples intentionally created terra preta dark-earth soils for large-scale agriculture over millennia, fundamentally reshaping forest composition.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream archaeology frames Amazon domestication as incremental cultivation, not deliberate wholesale construction; vast areas of the forest show no pre-Columbian human modification.

sources.title: MKUltra - Wikipedia
Unethical human experimentation in the United States - Wikipedia
The CIA's Appalling Human Experiments With Mind Control

evidence_for.text: MKUltra (1953-1964) is a documented CIA program that secretly dosed civilians and mental patients with LSD and other agents through at least 80 institutions without consent.
US government programs including Operation Sea-Spray conducted documented open-air biological agent tests on civilian populations in San Francisco.

sources.title: Friday Weird Science: The Concrete Enema
Concrete Enemas: An Unconventional and Dangerous Practice

evidence_against.text: The documented medical case was attributed to consensual sexual activity between adults; no clustering of such cases or secret society connection has been reported in medical literature.

research_note: fringe spiritual-cosmological noosphere variant; no external secular or academic coverage found; spiritual community lore only

sources.title: Religious war - Wikipedia
Religious Wars and Religious Freedom: A Troubled History

evidence_for.text: Documented conquests including Tudor suppression of Catholic Irish spiritual practices and Antiochus IV's banning of Jewish rites (167 BCE) show deliberate targeting of religion to break population resistance.

sources.title: Social isolation impairs adult neurogenesis in the limbic system and alters behaviors
How social isolation transforms the brain

evidence_for.text: Peer-reviewed research confirms social isolation activates dysfunction in the limbic system and ventral striatum, increasing anxiety-like and aggressive behaviors in isolated subjects.

evidence_against.text: Neuroscience shows isolation affects limbic function as part of an integrated network, not a singular takeover; effects vary significantly by individual, duration, and species.

sources.title: How The 'NPC' Meme Tries To Dehumanize 'SJWs'
Reincarnation and world population - Do the numbers add up?

evidence_against.text: The claim that population growth outruns available souls is an arithmetic myth; the number who died in the last century alone exceeds current living population.
The NPC-soul theory originated from a 4chan post combining bogus physics and speculative religion, spreading as a dehumanizing meme rather than a serious spiritual framework.

sources.title: A Few Words About The Flyers
Inorganic beings - Riverwalker Press

research_note: fringe biblical hermeneutic theory; no external secular or academic coverage found; niche theological speculation only

sources.title: How The 'NPC' Meme Tries To Dehumanize 'SJWs'
Can Demons Surf The Web? Exploring Digital Demonic Possibilities
A Demon in a Box? Unspooling the Dark Mythology of AI

evidence_against.text: Catholic theological analysis attributes smartphone-related harms to human design choices rather than demonic agency, absent specific evidence for supernatural causation.

sources.title: The Road to Roota Theory
Who Is Bix Weir of Road to Roota?

evidence_for.text: Bix Weir's theory centers on a Federal Reserve Bank of Boston comic book (Wishes and Rainbows, 1981) interpreted as a coded document signaling insider plans for a gold/silver monetary reset.

sources.title: Psychedelics alter metaphysical beliefs
Psychedelic experience - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: A peer-reviewed Scientific Reports study found psychedelic use significantly shifted metaphysical beliefs away from materialism toward panpsychism and fatalism, with changes lasting at least 6 months.

evidence_against.text: Psychedelic researchers attribute altered perception to serotonin receptor agonism rather than access to a separate spiritual dimension; effects are consistent with documented hallucination mechanics.

sources.title: Forbidden Archeology - Wikipedia
The human skull that challenges the Out of Africa theory

evidence_against.text: Mainstream scholars describe Forbidden Archeology as pseudoscience; ongoing legitimate fossil and genetic discoveries are actively expanding rather than suppressing the proto-human evolutionary record.

sources.title: Ancient astronauts - Wikipedia
Pseudoarchaeology and the ancient astronaut theory

evidence_against.text: Scientific consensus holds the ancient astronaut hypothesis is 'unjustified and unnecessary'; critics note it consistently underestimates documented human capabilities in archaeology and genetics.

research_note: no external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia
Thoughtforms

evidence_against.text: 76.5% of tulpa community practitioners attribute the phenomenon to neurological or psychological mechanisms; no empirical evidence supports mass fear of surveillance manifesting real supernatural surveillance entities.
